title: 🧩subworkflow - aws get host data of ec2 instance (gen2 aws connector)
description:
Subworkflow to get data of an aws ec2 instance without needing to know dql

An easy way to get additional data of an aws host
such as instance id, volume id etc. which is needed
to for example resize an aws volume.

Parameters:

host_entity_id: (Required) Dynatrace Host Entity Id
guide:
## 🧾 **Subworkflow to Get AWS EC2 Instance Data (No DQL Required)**

🔍 This subworkflow provides an **easy and DQL‑free way** to retrieve additional metadata for an AWS EC2 instance from Dynatrace.

☁️ The integration leverages the **Dynatrace AWS Gen2 connector**, ensuring secure, scalable, and modern access to AWS infrastructure metadata.

It is commonly used to collect information such as:
- 🆔 EC2 instance ID  
- 💽 Attached volume IDs  
- 📦 Other host-level metadata  

➡️ This data is typically required for follow-up automation steps, such as **resizing AWS EBS volumes** or performing infrastructure remediation.

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **host_entity_id** (Required): 🖥️ The Dynatrace **Host Entity ID** representing the AWS EC2 instance.

---

### 🧠 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Uses the **Dynatrace AWS Gen2 connector**
- ✅ Eliminates the need to write **DQL queries**
- ✅ Simplifies downstream automation logic
- ✅ Designed as a **helper / data-enrichment subworkflow**
- ✅ Commonly paired with volume extension, remediation, or validation workflows

---

input.query:
fetch dt.entity.host
| filter matchesPhrase(id, "{{input()["host_entity_id"]}}")
| fieldsadd  additionalSystemInfo,belongs_to,ec2_id=runs_on[dt.entity.ec2_instance], entity.name, hostname=entity.detected_name, osArchitecture, osType, cpuCores, autoInjection, bitness, cloudType, clustered_by,  ebpfHasPublicTraffic,  state
| lookup  [fetch dt.entity.ebs_volume
| fieldsadd arn,instanceid=belongs_to[dt.entity.ec2_instance][0],deviceName,ebsType,entity.name,awsNameTag,tags,belongs_to,boshName,creationTimestamp, lifetime, ebsSnapshotId, entity.type, iops  ] , sourceField:ec2_id, lookupField:instanceid, prefix:"ebs_"
| fieldsadd volumeid=ebs_entity.name
| lookup  [fetch dt.entity.ec2_instance
| fieldsadd awscredentials=accessible_by[dt.entity.aws_credentials], awsInstanceId,awsInstanceType,awsNameTag, awsSecurityGroup, awsVpcName, arn, tags, id, regionName, accessible_by,amiId,balanced_by,beanstalkEnvironmentName,belongs_to,boshName,contains,publicHostName,publicIp,localIp,virtualizedDiskType,entity.name,lifetime, entity.type  ] , sourceField:ec2_id, lookupField:id, prefix:"ec2_"
| lookup  [ timeseries max(dt.host.disk.used), by: { dt.entity.host }
| fieldsAdd disksizex = arrayLast(`max(dt.host.disk.used)`)/1000000000
| fields disksize=round(disksizex), dt.entity.host
] , sourceField:id, lookupField:dt.entity.host, prefix:"disk_"
| lookup  [ timeseries max(dt.host.disk.avail), by: { dt.entity.host }
| fieldsAdd disksizex = arrayLast(`max(dt.host.disk.avail)`)/1000000000
| fields disksize=round(disksizex), dt.entity.host
] , sourceField:id, lookupField:dt.entity.host, prefix:"disk_avail_"
| fieldsadd total_disk_size=disk_disksize + disk_avail_disksize, disk_used=disk_disksize, disk_available=disk_avail_disksize
| lookup  [ fetch dt.entity.aws_credentials | fields awsAccountId, aws_connection_name=entity.name, id ] , sourceField:ec2_awscredentials[0], lookupField:id, prefix:"conn_"
| fieldsremove disk_avail_disksize,disk_disksize
| fieldsadd conn_awsAccountId, conn_aws_connection_name
